questions.question: Which of the following was known as Bhinar?
questions.explanation: Ahmed Bahri Nizarn Shah founded the capital city of Ahmednagar in the latter part of his reign. It was known as Bhinar in early Yadava times, the new capital of the Nizam Shahis was named after Ahmed Bahri Nizarn Shah.

questions.question: Who was Adil Shahi ruler when independent Maratha State was founded?
questions.explanation: Shivaji founded the Maratha State during Muhammad Adil Shah’s reign. Adil Shah, known for the Gol Gumbaz and Ibrahim Rauza, faced Maratha challenges. Shivaji’s father, Shahaji, revolted after serving Bijapur. Shivaji used guerrilla warfare and forts like Torna and Rajgad to establish an independent kingdom.

questions.question: Who was the revenue collector during Mughal rule?
questions.explanation: Faujdar’s duty was to maintain law and order and safeguard the life and property of the residents of the areas under his jurisdiction. The Amalguzar or Amil was the revenue collector. He used to assess and supervise the revenue collection.

questions.question: Which of the following were given in lieu of salaries and they were transferable every three to four years?
questions.explanation: There were various types of jagirs in the Jagirdari dari syatem. The Tankha Jagirs were the ones which were given in lieu of salaries and they were transferable every three to four years.

questions.question: Which of the following was the Finance minister during the reign of Akbar?
questions.explanation: Raja Todar Mal who was one of the courtiers who were collectively known as Akbar’s Navratnas was also the finance minister under the reign of Akbar. He overlooked revenue system.

questions.question: Which of the following patronised scholars like Ghiyas Beg, Naqib Khan, and Niamatullah?
questions.explanation: During Jahangir’s reign, Abdul Hamid Lahori wrote *Padshah Namah*, and Khafi Khan wrote *Muntakhab-i-Lubab*. He patronized scholars like Ghiyas Beg and Naqib Khan, and his court attracted artists like Ustad Mansur and English ambassador Sir Thomas Roe. Jahangir commissioned *Tuzuk-i-Jahangiri*, detailing natural history, and his reign saw the flourishing of Persian literature and translations of Sanskrit texts.

questions.question: Which of the following gave the title of Raja to Shivaji?
questions.explanation: Shivaji Raje Bhonsle visited Agra with his son, faced humiliation from Aurangzeb, and escaped disguised as a palanquin bearer. Aurangzeb granted him the title of Raja and the Jagir of Berar. Shivaji hid in baskets of sweets during his escape and later established a strong Maratha navy. His coronation in 1674 as Chhatrapati marked his sovereignty and independence from Mughal rule.

questions.question: Which of the following were the ‘karkuns’ under Marathas?
questions.explanation: The Maratha King, Shivaji, drastically reduced the powers of the existing Deshmukhs and Kulkarnis. Shivaji also appointed his own revenue officials who were known as karkuns. Shivaji strongly discouraged revenue farming.

questions.question: What was the capital of Bahmani Kingdom?
questions.explanation: The Bahmani Kingdom, founded in 1347, initially had its capital at Gulbarga, known for its Indo-Islamic architecture and the Jama Masjid. In 1425, the capital moved to Bidar, a cultural center with Bidar Fort and Mahmud Gawan Madrasa. The kingdom significantly influenced Deccan politics and culture.

questions.question: Araghatta and Ghatiyantra are what kind of devices used in agriculture during the early medieval period?
questions.explanation: During early medieval period there were more and more sources of irrigation such as canals, lakes, cisterns, wells and stepped wells. The availability of water resources contributed to the expansion of cultivation and the intensification of agricultural activities. Water-lifting devices such as araghatta and ghatiyantra are mentioned in inscriptions and literary works.
